description_long: This object is a pair of long-nose pliers featuring elongated, tapered jaws made of metallic material, ideal for gripping, bending, and cutting wire in tight spaces. The jaws are equipped with a serrated gripping surface and a built-in wire cutter near the pivot. The handles are covered in a dual-color, rubberized grip—primarily yellow with gray accents—providing comfort and slip resistance during use. The pivot joint is robust and centrally located, allowing smooth opening and closing of the jaws. The overall construction suggests durability and suitability for electrical, mechanical, or general repair tasks. No visible branding or logo is present on the tool.
description: A pair of long-nose pliers with yellow and gray rubberized handles and tapered, serrated metal jaws, featuring a built-in wire cutter.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the pliers, highlighting the elongated jaws and dual-color handles.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the narrow, pointed jaws and the symmetrical, ergonomic handles.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, showing the other profile of the pliers and the handle contours.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the handles' curvature and the alignment of the jaws from behind.
attribute_handle_type: Ergonomic, straight handles with a slight outward curve, covered in yellow and gray rubberized insulation for comfort and slip resistance.
attribute_jaw_design: Long, tapered needle-nose jaws with serrated gripping surfaces and an integrated wire cutter near the pivot; designed for precision work in confined spaces.
attribute_pivot_mechanism: Fixed central pivot joint, providing a single, non-adjustable jaw width and smooth scissor-like movement.
attribute_grip_pattern: Rubberized handles with a smooth yet slightly textured surface for enhanced grip, featuring subtle molded contours for finger placement.
